---
title: "About Me"
---

I am a mathematics master's student at the University of Münster, working on my thesis with Urs Hartl. I graduated from Rose&ndash;Hulman Institute of Technology in May 2024 with a major in mathematics and a minor in statistics. I completed [my senior thesis](Senior%20Thesis.pdf) on Euler systems of number fields under the direction of [Tim All](https://www.rose-hulman.edu/~all1/). It was my privilege to learn a variety of topics from him, including real analysis, *p*-adic analysis, and algebraic topology, and I owe much of my mathematical development to his guidance.

My main mathematical interests broadly lie in algebraic number theory and arithmetic algebraic geometry. I am especially interested in the arithmetic of function fields (specifically in the style of Goss), a beautiful intersection of number theory, algebraic geometry, (nonarchimedean) analysis, and analytic geometry. In Goss's work, essentially all objects studied are in characteristic *p*: values of *L*-functions, class modules, Drinfeld modules and modular forms. Despite the change in setting, we still find many striking parallels to the classical setting of algebraic number theory and arithmetic geometry with complex-valued *L*-functions and representations.

In addition to math, I also enjoy travelling and playing the clarinet, and I had the honor of performing Mozart's *Clarinet Concerto in A Major* with the Rose-Hulman Symphony Orchestra in May 2024.


![The best part of travelling is seeing creatures like mama bear and baby bear](images/bears.JPG)
